devotions.title: Hudson Taylor's Faith-Filled Provision
devotions.scripture: Philippians 4:19 - And my God will supply every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.
devotions.story:
In 1854, Hudson Taylor arrived in China with no guaranteed support, trusting God alone for provision.. One winter, his mission faced a dire shortage.. He had just enough money to feed his team for one more day.. That evening, a letter arrived containing a £10 note—exactly the amount needed for the next month's food

But inside the envelope was only a check, no return address.. Taylor wept, realizing God's timing and sufficiency.. Years later, he wrote, "When I cannot trace His hand, I can trust His heart." This incident shaped his entire ministry: he never asked for money, only prayed, and God provided through unexpected donors, often at the last moment.. By the time he died in 1905, the China Inland Mission had over 800 missionaries

Taylor's life proves that God's provision is not about our abundance but His faithfulness.. He challenges us to step out in faith, trusting that the One who calls us will also provide every need.
devotions.prayer: Father, thank You for being our faithful provider. Teach us to trust You like Hudson Taylor, even when we cannot see the way. Help us to rely on Your timing and sufficiency, knowing You will meet every need according to Your riches. In Jesus' name, Amen.

devotions.title: Delivered from Darkness: The Story of John Newton
devotions.scripture: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and broke their bonds in pieces. — Psalm 107:14 (ESV)
devotions.story:
John Newton, once a slave trader and a man lost in sin, experienced a radical deliverance that transformed his life.. In 1748, during a violent storm at sea, Newton cried out to God for mercy.. Though he had mocked Christianity for years, that desperate prayer marked the beginning of his salvation.. He later wrote, “I was a wretch, and a blasphemer, and a persecutor, but I obtained mercy.” After his conversion, Newton left the slave trade and became a pastor and hymn writer, penning the beloved song “Amazing Grace.” His life became a testimony of God's power to deliver anyone from the deepest bondage—whether physical, spiritual, or moral

Newton spent the rest of his days preaching grace and fighting against slavery, showing that true deliverance leads to a life of service and love.. His story reminds us that no one is beyond the reach of God's saving hand.
devotions.prayer: Lord, thank You for delivering us from sin and death. Like John Newton, may we never forget the grace that saved us. Help us to live as testimonies of Your deliverance, and use us to bring freedom to others in bondage. In Jesus' name, Amen.

devotions.title: Forgiveness Devotion - Friday, June 26, 2026
devotions.scripture: "Trust in the Lord with all your heart." — Proverbs 3:5
devotions.story: A reflection on Forgiveness.. This is a story about faith and trust in God.. Even in difficult times, we can find hope and strength through our faith.. The journey of faith is not always easy, but God is with us every step of the way.
devotions.prayer: Lord, help us to trust in You and to walk in faith. Amen.

devotions.title: Guided and Guarded: Gladys Aylward's Journey of Protection
devotions.scripture: For he will command his angels concerning you to guard you in all your ways. (Psalm 91:11)
devotions.story:
Gladys Aylward, a British missionary, felt called to China in the 1930s.. With no formal training and limited funds, she traveled alone across Siberia and through war-torn regions to reach the small village of Yangcheng.. There, she became known as ‘Ai-Weh-Deh’ (The Virtuous One) and adopted orphaned children.. During the Sino-Japanese War, Japanese forces advanced towards her village

Gladys faced a impossible choice: leave the 100 children she had rescued to face certain death, or lead them to safety across the mountains.. With unwavering trust in God, she chose to flee.. For 12 days, Gladys and the children walked through harsh terrain, often by night, relying on God’s protection.. When food ran out, locals miraculously provided rice

When she feared capture, enemy patrols inexplicably passed by.. At one point, a young child collapsed from exhaustion; Gladys prayed fervently, and the child revived.. They finally crossed the Yellow River into Free China.. Gladys later said, ‘I did not lead them—God did.’ Her faith in divine protection turned a desperate journey into a testament of God’s unwavering care.
devotions.prayer: Heavenly Father, thank You for being our shield and protector. Like Gladys Aylward, help us to trust Your guidance even when the path seems dangerous. Guard us in our daily walk and give us courage to protect others in Your name. In Jesus’ name, Amen.

devotions.title: Wisdom from Above: Augustine's Turning Point
devotions.scripture: James 1:5 (NIV) – 'If any of you lacks wisdom, you should ask God, who gives generously to all without finding fault, and it will be given to you.'
devotions.story:
Augustine of Hippo, born in 354 AD in Roman North Africa, was a brilliant scholar who sought wisdom in worldly pleasures and philosophies.. For years, he lived a life of moral compromise, despite the fervent prayers of his mother, Monica.. He taught rhetoric in Carthage, Rome, and Milan, yet his soul remained restless.. One day, in a Milan garden, he heard a child’s voice chanting, “Take up and read.” Interpreting it as divine instruction, he opened the Scriptures to Romans 13:13-14, which commanded him to clothe himself with Christ

In that moment, God granted him the wisdom to leave his old ways and fully surrender to Jesus.. Augustine later became a bishop, theologian, and one of Christianity’s greatest thinkers.. His writings, such as *Confessions*, overflow with gratitude for God’s wisdom that transformed his life.. He realized that true wisdom is not human cleverness but the gift of knowing and loving God through Christ

His story reminds us that when we lack wisdom, we can ask our generous Father, who is eager to give it.
devotions.prayer: Father, we thank You for the wisdom that only You can give. Help us, like Augustine, to turn from our own understanding and humbly ask for Your guidance. May Your Word and Spirit direct our steps each day. In Jesus’ name, Amen.

devotions.title: Ruth's Loyal Love: A Covenant of Faith
devotions.story:
Ruth, a Moabite widow, faced a devastating choice.. Her mother-in-law Naomi, also widowed, urged her to return to her own people and gods.. But Ruth’s love was not mere affection—it was a courageous, sacrificial covenant.. She declared, “Where you go I will go, and where you stay I will stay

Your people will be my people and your God my God.” This was no empty promise; Ruth left everything she knew—her homeland, family, and culture—to follow Naomi into an uncertain future in Bethlehem.. Poverty, danger, and rejection awaited her as a foreigner.. Yet Ruth’s love was rooted in her newfound faith in the God of Israel.. She didn’t just cling to Naomi; she clung to God

In Bethlehem, she worked tirelessly in the fields, gleaning grain to provide for them both.. Her humility and loyalty caught the eye of Boaz, a wealthy relative who became her kinsman-redeemer.. Ruth’s love story transcends romance—it demonstrates agape love, the selfless, God-centered love that risks everything for another.. Through her faithfulness, God wove her into the lineage of King David and ultimately Jesus Christ

Ruth’s love teaches us that true devotion involves leaving our comfort zones and trusting God’s sovereign plan, even when the path is unclear.
devotions.prayer: Father, like Ruth, help us to love with a loyal, sacrificial love that reflects Your heart. Give us courage to leave behind what is familiar and to trust You in the unknown. May our love for others be rooted in our love for You, and may we be a blessing as Ruth was. In Jesus’ name, Amen.

devotions.title: The Waiting Harvest: William Carey's Patience in India
devotions.scripture: Be patient, therefore, brothers, until the coming of the Lord. See how the farmer waits for the precious fruit of the earth, being patient about it, until it receives the early and the late rains. — James 5:7
devotions.story:
William Carey, the father of modern missions, arrived in India in 1793 with a burning desire to translate the Bible and see souls saved.. But for seven long years, he faced nothing but rejection, disease, and financial hardship.. His wife struggled with mental illness, and fellow missionaries abandoned him.. Many mocked his efforts, calling him a fool

Yet Carey did not give up.. He learned Bengali, Sanskrit, and Marathi, laboring daily on translation.. In 1800, after seven years of patient toil, the first Bengali convert—Krishna Pal—was baptized.. This small harvest sparked a movement

Carey would go on to translate Scripture into over 40 languages and establish Serampore College.. His patience was not passive waiting; it was active, faithful work, trusting God for fruit in due season.. As a farmer tends the soil, Carey cultivated the ground of India with prayer and perseverance, believing that God's promise would not return empty.
devotions.prayer: Lord, teach us to wait as William Carey did—not idly, but with faithful labor. When the harvest seems delayed, strengthen our hearts. Help us to trust Your timing and to sow in patience, knowing that You will bring the fruit in Your season. Amen.
